content_of_post_to_comment:
Mine was replying to a two-line email.

It would've taken less than a minute.

Instead, I avoided it for four days.

Executive dysfunction makes the smallest tasks feel strangely impossible.

What's yours?
comment: The two-line email is painfully relatable because it's rarely the email itself. Mine is sending a simple text I've already written in my head. Somehow the tiny tasks have the least urgency but create the most background stress. Then once I finally do it, I wonder what I was avoiding.
